checks.name: content-free telemetry boundary
checks.summary: Only bounded route names, closed enums, irreversible correlation hashes, canonical trace identifiers, and approved resource attributes can be exported; raw task, output, SQL, credential, error, baggage, and tracestate content are excluded.

checks.name: fail-soft authority isolation
checks.summary: Telemetry exporters, collectors, dashboards, and alerts are disposable derived views; failures cannot authorize work, mutate PostgreSQL, or roll back a valid canonical transition.

checks.name: bounded local exposure
checks.summary: Daemon metrics and development observability endpoints bind to host loopback, metric gathers are serialized, and PostgreSQL snapshots use bounded timeouts and content-free counts.

checks.name: pinned and isolated observability stack
checks.summary: Every image is version-pinned, Alloy positions persist in a dedicated volume, and each destructive smoke rehearsal uses a unique Compose project.

checks.name: tenant and cardinality boundaries
checks.summary: Operational SQL remains tenant and repository scoped; Prometheus and Loki indexed labels exclude tenant, repository, run, task, attempt, correlation, and causation identifiers.

residual_risks: Telemetry data and Alloy checkpoints are intentionally disposable operational metadata rather than durable evidence.
The bundled observability stack is a loopback-only development profile, not a hardened production deployment.
Unstructured stderr remains potentially sensitive and requires deployment-owned retention and deletion outside the ingested JSONL stream.
Durable large artifacts and long-term evidence retention remain Sprint 07 responsibilities.
A public scheduler or MCP command for the complete delivery pipeline remains outside Sprint 06.
